title: XM Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of XM pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
XM Pricing Overview

XM (Trading Point Holdings) operates a refreshingly straightforward pricing model: no account maintenance fees, no monthly subscriptions, and no hidden charges. The broker makes money from trading spreads and commissions, not from keeping your account open. With a $5 minimum deposit to start trading micro accounts, XM positions itself as one of the most accessible forex brokers globally, catering to beginners and small-account traders who want to learn without significant financial risk.

Founded in 2009 and regulated in Cyprus, XM has attracted over 10 million clients worldwide by combining low barriers to entry with industry-leading education and research tools. If you're evaluating whether XM fits your budget, the answer largely depends on your trading style, account size, and which markets you plan to trade.

All XM Plans Compared

XM offers three primary account types, all free to open. The differences lie in leverage, spreads, and execution model rather than subscription tiers:

Account Type Account Opening Fee Minimum Deposit Max Leverage Spreads (EUR/USD) Commission
Micro $0 $5 1:888 1.2 pips (approx.) None
Standard $0 $100 1:888 1.5 pips (approx.) None
XM Zero $0 $100 1:500 0 pips base + $10 commission per 1M $10 per 1M volume

Micro Account: The entry-level option. With spreads starting around 1.2 pips on major pairs and micro lot sizing (0.01 lots = $0.10 per pip), you can trade with minimal capital. Leverage up to 1:888 means you can control larger positions, but position sizing remains conservative—ideal for learning and paper trading without excessive risk.

Standard Account: The most popular choice for retail traders. Slightly wider spreads (~1.5 pips) than Micro, but you get access to the same 1000+ instruments and educational tools. Minimum deposit of $100 signals this is for traders ready to risk real money beyond the micro-scale.

XM Zero Account: Built for cost-conscious traders who want ultra-tight spreads. The EUR/USD spread drops to zero, but you pay a $10 commission per 1 million volume traded. This model suits active scalpers and day traders who'd otherwise pay more in spread costs. The trade-off is slightly lower leverage (1:500 vs 1:888).

Monthly and Annual Costs: XM charges no monthly or annual subscription fees. You only pay when you trade—through spreads on Micro/Standard accounts or spreads + commissions on XM Zero.

Free Plan / Free Trial

XM does not offer a traditional "free trial" with real money, but it provides two risk-free ways to evaluate the platform:

  • Demo Account: Unlimited practice account with $100,000 virtual funds. No time limit. Perfect for testing strategies, learning the platform, and practicing trade execution without risking capital.
  • Micro Account with $5: The closest thing to a "free tier" is opening a Micro account and depositing just $5. While not free, the barrier is so low that you can test live conditions and the platform's execution at near-zero risk.

Is the Free Option Worth It? Yes. XM's demo account is genuinely unlimited, and the educational resources (webinars, video courses, trading guides) are free to access without opening an account. If you're hesitant about real money, start with the demo and pair it with XM's free education to build confidence before depositing.

Hidden Costs and Fees

XM's pricing is relatively transparent, but here are costs beyond the base spread/commission:

  • Withdrawal Fees: Most withdrawal methods are free, but processing can take 2–5 business days. Some e-wallet withdrawals may carry third-party charges depending on your payment provider.
  • Inactivity Fee: Accounts inactive for 90 days may incur a monthly $5 fee. Keep your account active with at least one trade per quarter to avoid this.
  • Negative Balance Protection: Free feature that protects you from losing more than your deposit (a regulatory requirement in many jurisdictions). No hidden charge here—it's a pro, not a con.
  • Cryptocurrency Trading: Spreads on crypto pairs are wider than forex (e.g., Bitcoin pairs may have 100+ pips). If crypto is your focus, this platform isn't optimal for cost-sensitive traders.
  • Data Feeds and News: XM includes real-time market data, economic calendars, and research reports with all accounts at no extra cost.

Swap Costs: If you hold positions overnight, you'll pay or receive swap rates (interest adjustments based on the interest rate differential between currency pairs). This is standard across all brokers and isn't a "hidden" cost, but it's worth factoring into long-term position costs.

XM Pricing vs Competitors

How does XM stack up against other global forex brokers? Here's a realistic comparison on EUR/USD spreads:

Broker Standard Spread (EUR/USD) Minimum Deposit Commission Model
XM Micro ~1.2 pips $5 Spread only
OANDA ~1.0 pips $1 Spread only
IG ~0.8 pips $250 Spread + commissions on some accounts
Interactive Brokers ~0.1 pips $100 $2–4 per 100K volume
Pepperstone ~0.1 pips $200 $3.50 per lot

The Verdict: XM's spreads are competitive but not the tightest. OANDA and Interactive Brokers offer tighter EUR/USD spreads, but Interactive Brokers requires $100 minimum and more active trading to justify commissions. XM wins on accessibility (lowest minimum deposit) and for beginners who value education alongside trading. If you're a high-volume trader or scalper, Pepperstone or Interactive Brokers offer better per-trade costs despite higher spreads.

Is XM Worth the Price?

For Forex Beginners: Absolutely. The $5 micro account entry point, combined with 1:888 leverage and free education, makes XM one of the best platforms for learning forex without risking significant capital. You'll pay slightly above-market spreads (1.2 pips on Micro vs ~1.0 pips elsewhere), but the learning advantage justifies it.

For Small Account Traders ($100–$5,000): XM Standard is solid value. You get no monthly fees, reasonable spreads, and access to 1000+ instruments. Your profit margin will be smaller due to wider spreads, but costs stay predictable.

For Active Day Traders: Consider XM Zero if you trade daily. The zero-spread model with $10/million commission can be cheaper than paying 1.5-pips on every micro account trade, especially at high volume. The trade-off is slightly lower leverage (1:500).

For Cryptocurrency Traders: XM is not ideal. Wide crypto spreads (100+ pips on Bitcoin) make it expensive compared to dedicated crypto exchanges or brokers specializing in digital assets.

Upgrading Economics: Start with Micro to prove your strategy. Once you're consistently profitable and ready to deploy more capital ($500+), upgrade to Standard for better execution. Move to XM Zero only if you're executing 10+ trades per week and can justify the commission structure.

How to Save on XM

While XM doesn't advertise discounts or promo codes directly, here are legitimate ways to reduce costs:

  • Use the Demo Account First: Eliminate the learning phase in live markets. Perfect your strategy in the demo before risking real deposits.
  • Choose the Right Account Type: Match your trading frequency to your account type. Micro for testing, Standard for consistent traders, Zero for high-volume active traders.
  • Avoid Overnight Holds: Minimize swap costs by closing positions within the same day when possible, especially on less liquid pairs.
  • Keep Your Account Active: Trade at least once every 90 days to avoid the $5/month inactivity fee.
  • Refer Friends: XM offers referral commissions. If you refer another trader, both you and the new account holder receive rebates (typically $5–$10 per referred lot traded).
  • Leverage Deposits Strategically: Use XM's high leverage (1:888 on Micro) to control larger positions with smaller deposits, reducing your capital at risk—though this increases loss risk if not managed carefully.

XM's 10+ million-client base and 4.1/5 rating reflect genuine value for the price point, especially for traders prioritizing education and accessibility. The platform earns trust through transparent pricing and negative balance protection, a critical feature for risk management.

Ready to dive deeper? Read our full XM review or compare XM to other forex brokers to find the best fit for your trading style and budget.
